event: acute coronary syndromes within a 1-month interval
admitted to cardiology department for acute thoracic pain in May 2013
electrocardiogram evidenced inferior Q waves with ST elevation
coronary catheterization revealed intraluminal thrombus in right coronary artery
no stent inserted due to unexplained fever
acute chest pain one month later
asymptomatic incomplete intraluminal obstruction of circumflex artery
angioplasty performed without stent due to unexplained fever
tests for systemic inflammatory diseases negative
CT scan showed thrombosis of hepatic vein
PNH clone with 80% neutrophils lacking CD66b, CD16, CD24
postponed eculizumab due to fever and infection risk
diagnosis of refractory anemia with excess blast-2
admitted to intensive care unit for septic shock
infection of implantable chamber by Aeromonas veronii
